od_description: Soura ↔ central Srinagar (Munawar Abad; a short spur toward Rainawari). A Soura radial running the shared central spine — not a distinct clean O→D.
stops_verdict: Strong, driver-corroborated stops along the Soura–central spine (2500/29 at Soura; 1515/24, 928/18, 738/33 centrally). The Rainawari-end stop (34.09891,74.82903) has only 1 driver — a representative-run artifact, NOT a real terminal.
narrative: Another Soura radial. The observed line is the same Soura↔central-Srinagar trunk shared by several permits; the terminal-DBSCAN picked a medoid ending at a Rainawari spur, making it look like a distinct O→D. No candidate is 'Soura–Rainawari'; the top overlaps (Chanapora–Soura 0.70, Rangpora–Dalgate 0.68, Soura–Lalchowk 0.65) all just share the Soura trunk. Best single association is FDR-078 (shares the Soura terminal) but the assignment is genuinely ambiguous — this is a shared trunk, not one permit.
evidence_used: geocoded endpoints: Soura (A), Rainawari (B, weak/1-driver), Munawar Abad (mid)
map: green line is the Soura–central spine with a minor east spur to Rainawari; overlaps several radials but matches none end-to-end
data_quality_flags: corridor-clustering fragmentation: near the core, one busy Soura↔central trunk is split into several 'corridors' (cf. C1) by medoid endpoint spurs — aggregate should merge these
implied_headway 710 min is a partial-adoption artifact
analyst_note: Meta-finding for aggregation: several top core corridors are variants of the same Soura↔central spine; consider merging by shared trunk, not just terminal pair.
